hypothesis: If an EGNN-based SDE diffusion model for atomic positions and element embeddings is trained on amorphous materials and augmented with energy-based scoring plus HMC refinement (AMDEN), then it will generate amorphous silicon and multicomponent glass structures whose RDFs, bond-angle distributions, structure factors, and energy distributions match MD references and, when conditioned on Young’s modulus, achieve target moduli because equivariant score matching with physics-guided refinement captures the underlying energy landscape and property–structure relationships.
